concepts.title: HTTP Request Methods
concepts.description: HTTP has several request methods, including GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc. Understanding the difference between these methods is crucial, as each serves a specific purpose in web development.

concepts.title: Web Page Structure
concepts.description: A web page consists of content, structure, and presentation. HTML is used for structure, CSS for presentation, and JavaScript for dynamic content. Remembering this hierarchy is essential for web development.

concepts.title: HTML Tags and Attributes
concepts.description: Familiarity with common HTML tags and attributes is frequently tested. Knowing the difference between block-level and inline elements, as well as the purpose of attributes like href, src, and alt, is vital.

concepts.title: HTTP Status Codes
concepts.description: To remember common HTTP status codes, use the mnemonic 'Oh No, Cats Eating Dinner': 200 OK, 301 Moved, 401 Unauthorized, 404 Not Found. This helps in recalling the most frequently used status codes.

concepts.title: Confusing HTML and XHTML
concepts.description: Students often confuse HTML and XHTML. Remember that XHTML is a stricter version of HTML, requiring all tags to be closed and following XML syntax rules. Avoid using XHTML-specific features in HTML documents.

concepts.title: Color Representation in Web Development
concepts.description: In web development, colors can be represented using RGB (Red, Green, Blue) values, hexadecimal codes, or color names. Remembering the basic color codes and how to convert between these representations is necessary.

concepts.title: JavaScript Basics
concepts.description: JavaScript is a client-side scripting language used for creating dynamic web pages. Understanding variables, data types, functions, loops, and conditional statements in JavaScript is fundamental for web development.

concepts.title: Web Development Tools
concepts.description: Familiarity with web development tools like text editors, IDEs, and browsers is essential. Knowing how to use the developer tools in browsers for debugging and inspecting web pages is also frequently tested.
